sourceMovie.title: Bunraku
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Bunraku (2010) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Guy Moshe's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bunraku clicked for you, Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Jackal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bunraku clicked for you, The Jackal (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bunraku?
faq.answer: Try Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance, The Jackal, War—each shares DNA with Bunraku while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bunraku?
faq.answer: Bunraku (2010) is best described as Thriller, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bunraku worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
